reviewerRoles.focus: Natural Korean prose, child-friendly phrasing, vocabulary clarity, and narration rhythm.
Folktale-specific objects, settings, food, household details, character roles, and avoiding generic or flattened Korean culture.
English-first U.S. family use while keeping Korean terms, bilingual learning value, and bedtime comfort.
Age-appropriate suspense, non-graphic conflict, respectful modernization, and no horror/gore.

globalFindings: All 24 complete catalog books have story-specific folktale beats after the semantic authenticity repair pass.
The prior premium scaffold prose class is now guarded by `tools/validate_story_asset_authenticity.py`.
This remains internal demo review only, not external human cultural/editorial/child-safety approval.
Generated art and synthetic narration are not final production assets.
approvalCriteria: Korean folktale identity is visible through food, household, clothing, seasonal, rural, and oral-story details.
Korean terms are either natural in context or explained with child-friendly vocabulary definitions.
English story text is not a stiff literal translation.
Suspense and humor are story-specific, child-safe, and not flattened into generic kindness.
The three tiger stories use distinct tiger personalities.
Modernized endings avoid graphic cruelty while preserving story stakes.
No copyrighted production style references are required for story or art direction.
Generated draft art and synthetic narration are not marked final.

notes: Repo-local semantic repair checks story-specific beats, sensitive emotional tone, metadata uniqueness, and child-safe adaptation boundaries.
External children’s editor, Korean-language, Korean cultural, and child-safety review remain required before final release.
requiredBeforeFinal: Child-safety adaptation review with comments resolved.
